channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Mixed Feelings as Iran Ties With New Zealand in World Cup
item.description: Iran and New Zealand played their World Cup opener on Monday, tying 2-2. Some spectators protested the Islamic Republic of Iran while others cheered on the team.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times

item.title: Millones de mujeres se quedan fuera de las terapias para la menopausia
item.description: Las mujeres que no pueden recibir terapia hormonal debido al cáncer de mama u otras enfermedades dicen sentirse profundamente frustradas.
item.dc:creator: Liz Krieger

media:content.media:credit: Jillian Freyer para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Maylone toma un medicamento que suprime el estrógeno para reducir el riesgo de que su cáncer reaparezca.

item.title: El presidente Trump perdió esta guerra
item.description: Estados Unidos sale más endeble —militar, diplomática y económicamente— de lo que estaba al inicio de los ataques en Medio Oriente.
item.dc:creator: El Comité Editorial
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Damon Winter/The New York Times

item.title: Live Updates: Ukraine and Middle East Are Focus as Group of 7 Leaders Meet
item.description: Arriving at the summit, President Trump sought to promote his preliminary deal with Iran. President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine was hoping to shift leaders’ attention to the war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times

item.title: ¿Qué pasa cuando el mayor experto en ‘deepfakes’ ya no confía en sus propios ojos?
item.description: Para Hany Farid, demostrar qué es real en la era de la IA es cada vez más complicado.
item.dc:creator: Eli Saslow and Erin Schaff
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Erin Schaff/The New York Times

item.title: Knicks Celebrate Their N.B.A. Title on ‘The Tonight Show’
item.description: “Of course it’s the Knicks, so they won’t really show up until the second half,” the host Jimmy Fallon said.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Mamdani Is Channeling Knicks’ Fans Euphoria. Will It Work?
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s campaign team used some viral Knicks fan videos as inspiration for his own video strategy to convey a sense of urgency and excitement.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Adam Gray/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Six months into his mayoralty, Mayor Zohran Mamdani is benefiting from the city’s embrace of the Knicks’ victory.

item.title: What’s in a Stadium Name? A World Cup Snub for New Jersey.
item.description: To comply with a FIFA prohibition on branding, MetLife Stadium in New Jersey was renamed “New York New Jersey Stadium.” New Jerseyans were not surprised.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Purdy

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: For the World Cup matches in the Meadowlands, signs for “New York New Jersey” hid what was MetLife Stadium just a few weeks ago.

item.title: The Knicks’ Championship Run, in 5 Voices
item.description: New Yorkers, from bartenders to teenagers to TV celebrities, turned the city orange and blue in an unusual but welcome sign of unity.
item.dc:creator: Davaughnia Wilson

media:content.media:credit: Kaja Andric for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kim Campbell gathered with other Knicks fans outside Madison Square Garden during the N.B.A. finals.

item.title: What’s the Best Way to Get to World Cup Games in New Jersey From NYC?
item.description: Soccer fans attending World Cup games have confusing and sometimes enraging transportation choices to make. Starting out in Midtown Manhattan, we tested each route.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen, Maria Cramer, Wm. Ferguson and Christopher Maag

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 16, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Word of the Day: mastication
item.description: This word has appeared in three articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: ¿Cómo puede un solo cerebro hablar dos idiomas?
item.description: Un nuevo estudio sobre personas bilingües sugiere que un único “motor gramatical” en el cerebro puede gestionar varias lenguas a la vez.
item.dc:creator: K. R. Callaway

item.title: This New Party Wants to Write a New Future for Israel
item.description: A new Israeli-Palestinian political party, A Place For Us All, was born out of joint activism and organizing focused on the daily realities on the ground, not grandiose policies.
item.dc:creator: Mairav Zonszein

media:content.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times

item.title: How Tehran Won the World
item.description: Iran’s defiance in the face of Western aggression has become a rallying cry.
item.dc:creator: Azadeh Moaveni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by Tam Stockton for The New York Times; source photograph by Vahid Salemi/Associated Press

item.title: Japan Raises Rates to 31-Year High to Ward Off War Inflation
item.description: Going against Prime Minister Takaichi’s wishes, Japan’s central bank acted amid U.S. pressure, a tanking currency and inflation from energy disruptions.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis and Kiuko Notoya

media:content.media:credit: Jiji Press/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi of Japan in Tokyo last month. Her government spending agenda faces pressure from rising interest rates.

item.title: Los mejores programas de televisión de 2026 hasta ahora
item.description: El año arrancó con pocos éxitos rotundos, pero hemos visto sorpresas agradables como ‘Widow’s Bay’, y el regreso de algunos de nuestros favoritos como ‘The Comeback’ y los Muppets.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Why Team Iran Is in a Tough Spot at the World Cup
item.description: At its first game in Los Angeles, the men’s national team drew spectators who weren’t coming for the soccer, but rather to protest the regime in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Fans holding Iran’s pre-revolutionary flag at SoFi Stadium near Los Angeles on Monday.

item.title: China’s Spending Slowdown Deepens as Households Tighten Their Belts
item.description: The unexpected decline in retail sales underscored China’s growing reliance on exports to sustain economic growth as domestic demand continues to falter.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher

media:content.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Retail sales dropped 0.6 percent in May from the same month a year earlier, the National Bureau of Statistics said on Tuesday.

item.title: Naval Mines Could Still Stymie Gulf Shipping After War
item.description: The U.S. Navy will be deploying a new generation of drone-based countermeasures to search the sea floor and surface for any mines Iran laid.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay and Nicholas Kulish
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: Vessels trapped in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Oman.

item.title: Australia’s Former Electricity Substations Are Being Turned Into Homes
item.description: In a country where the housing market is squeezed, decommissioned electrical substations are in demand as refurbished residences.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

media:content.media:credit: Adam Ferguson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Sam Thomlinson, left, conducting an open inspection at the Bondi Beach property.

item.title: La dignidad de Estados Unidos cayó a la lona
item.description: La noche de peleas del cumpleaños del presidente Trump en el jardín de la Casa Blanca fue un espectáculo absurdo, sí, pero también inquietante.
item.dc:creator: Will Leitch
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Mark Peterson para The New York Times

item.title: Quote of the Day: Russians Exploit Gap Over Ukraine
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, June 16, 2026

item.title: Here Are the 2026 James Beard Restaurant Award Winners
item.description: Kalaya took the outstanding restaurant award and Michael Tusk took home the outstanding chef honor during an evening when immigration and resistance emerged as themes.
item.dc:creator: Julia Moskin

media:content.media:credit: Jeff Schear/Getty Images for The James Beard
media:content.media:description: Michael Tusk, of Quince and Cotogna in San Francisco, was named outstanding chef at the James Beard Awards on Monday night.

item.title: The Fitness Dating App Boom
item.description: A slew of dating apps for fitness enthusiasts has recently hit the market. Danielle Friedman, a reporter for Well, interviewed people about why they’re drawn to these apps and what it says about the culture at large.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Friedman, Theodore Tae and Robert Gourley

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 16, 2026
item.description: Brad Lively makes an earnest effort.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: Sally Ryan for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Are you as tickled pink by a good crossword as we are?

item.title: How the Kratom Industry Won Friends in the Trump Administration
item.description: A sprawling influence campaign to promote a risky drug called kratom has gained allies among top Trump administration officials. Our investigative reporter Kenneth P. Vogel explains.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth P. Vogel, Paul Abowd, Jon Miller, Ray Whitehouse, Stephanie Swart and Rafaela Balster

item.title: Why President Trump Called Me About Iran
item.description: A day before the United States and Iran signed a framework agreement to end their monthslong war, President Trump called David E. Sanger, White House and national security correspondent for The Times, to discuss the arrangement.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Nikolay Nikolov, Alexandra Ostasiewicz, Rafaela Balster, Jon Hazell and Aritz Parra
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Attempted BASE Jump in Utah Kills 2, Including Acclaimed Extreme Athlete
item.description: Andrew Lewis, 39, best known for performing in the halftime show of a Super Bowl, was killed in the accident.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin

media:content.media:credit: Sakchai Lalit/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Andrew Lewis on a slackline in Bangkok in 2014. He participated in several extreme sports and ran an adventure sports business.

item.title: In the Dark on U.S.-Iran Deal, Senators Refrain From Praising It
item.description: Democrats demanded an immediate briefing and even Republicans conceded they had no information on an agreement the administration has declined to release.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison and Michael Gold

media:content.media:credit: Michael A. McCoy for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator John Thune of South Dakota, the majority leader, said that he had not yet seen the deal but said he expected the administration to convene with lawmakers as the process continues.

item.title: Netanyahu Says Israel Will Keep Forces in Lebanon, Despite U.S.-Iran Deal
item.description: “The struggle has not ended,” a defiant Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told Israelis.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Ronen Zvulun
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel speaking at a news conference in Jerusalem on Monday.

item.title: Newsom Says Trump Is Investigating Him and His Wife
item.description: In a video posted on social media on Monday, Gov. Gavin Newsom of California said President Trump used the Justice Department to investigate him and his wife, Jennifer Siebel Newsom.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: The UFC 250 Was a Garish Spectacle of American Decline
item.description: Only the hackiest screenwriter imaginable would script America’s debasement this way.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Goldberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Mark Peterson/Redux, for The New York Times

item.title: Read an FBI agent’s email about the impending arrest of Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: An FBI agent briefs colleagues that Epstein will be flying from Paris into Teterboro Airport in New Jersey, where they will attempt to arrest him upon his landing on July 6, 2019.

item.title: Britain Proposes Social Media Ban for Children Under 16
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain said that the government would introduce new regulations later this year to ban social media for children under 16. The measures, expected to take effect in early 2027, will also include restrictions on some game platforms and livestreaming apps.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:credit: Katie Collins/Reuters
media:content.media:description: High school students in Wimbledon, London, this year during an interview about social media. Britain plans to place an age limit on social media.

item.title: Montana Democrats Are Divided Over How to Win a Republican-held Senate Seat
item.description: A rift in Montana between the Democratic nominee and an independent candidate could boost Republican hopes of holding on to an open seat in conservative territory.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning and Jack Healy

media:content.media:credit: Alani Bankhead for Montana, Will Warasila for The New York Times, Thom Bridge/Independent Record, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Three candidates are vying for a Senate seat in Montana: Alani Bankhead, a Democrat, Seth Bodnar, an independent, and Kurt Alme, a Republican. Democrats fear Ms. Bankhead and Mr. Bodnar will split the vote of Democrats and independents.

item.title: Divider in Chief
item.description: Unlike past presidents, Donald Trump “uses everything to divide us.” America’s 250th anniversary is no exception, the NYT Opinion contributor Pete Wehner argues on “The Opinions.”

item.title: A Knicks-Inspired Baby-Naming Boom Is Coming After Championship Win, Experts Say
item.description: Jalen Brunson could become the next Michael Jordan of baby names, but don’t expect more Victors or Wembys, the experts say.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Southall

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “Jalen” is already a popular baby name, but Knicks-loving parents may turn to it even more now that Jalen Brunson led the team to an N.B.A. championship, experts say.

item.title: Scorching Heat or Scorching Lightning? Miami’s Match Could Have Both.
item.description: South Florida was under a severe heat advisory as fans arrived in the hours before kickoff. Scattered thunderstorms are also in the forecast.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei

media:content.media:credit: Marco Bello/Reuters
media:content.media:description: The Weather Service predicted possible thunderstorms near the start of the World Cup game on Monday in Miami.

item.title: The (Mostly) Unanswered Questions at the Heart of a U.S.-Iran Accord
item.description: The short-term truce hasn’t been made public, and the long-term peace deal it is supposed to yield doesn’t exist yet, so there is much still unknown about any agreement between the adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Richard Pérez-Peña
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump at Évian-les-Bains, France, for the Group of 7 summit, on Monday.

item.title: Copa Mundial: los fans de Cabo Verde celebran el empate contra España
item.description: Cabo Verde, una pequeña nación insular, es uno de los países más pequeños que han clasificado para un Mundial. Los fanáticos festejaron que el equipo resistiera ante uno de los favoritos del torneo.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas

media:content.media:credit: Brett Davis/Imagn, vía Reuters
media:content.media:description: Para los seguidores de la selección de Cabo Verde, el mero hecho de llegar al Mundial y jugar contra España, una de las mejores selecciones del mundo, fue como vivir un momento histórico.

item.title: En Israel, crece el descontento en torno al acuerdo entre EE. UU. e Irán
item.description: Israelíes de todo el espectro político dijeron que el acuerdo parece dejar sin resolver las amenazas fundamentales a la seguridad israelí que plantea Irán.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Foto de consorcio por Ronen Zvulun
media:content.media:description: El primer ministro Benjamín Netanyahu en marzo. Al inicio de la reciente guerra, dijo que su objetivo era “eliminar las amenazas existenciales” para Israel.

item.title: Trump Praises Peace Deal Framework With Iran
item.description: On Monday, the U.S. and Iran signed a preliminary peace deal, but the terms of the agreement remained a secret. While the U.S. and Iranian officials made conflicting statements about the deal’s terms, it was clear that the deal’s immediate scope was focused primarily on ending military attacks and allowing traffic to resume through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper and Jiawei Wang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tehran last week.

item.title: Terms of U.S.-Iran Deal Remain Secret
item.description: Cape Verde pulls off a shocker at the World Cup. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times

item.title: Potential End of War Tests Trump’s Promise of Quick Economic Rebound
item.description: Gas prices and other goods could remain elevated for months, adding to the political challenge facing the White House in the midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The United States and Iran have signed a framework agreement for ending the war, but neither side has published the full text and its details remain unknown.

item.title: Will Commercial Ships Have to Pay to Pass Through the Strait of Hormuz? Maybe.
item.description: Charging a toll is illegal under international law, but some fees are allowed for services. It is not clear what services Iran would provide, but there were no fees charged before the war.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, seen from Oman on Wednesday.

item.title: Kennedy Orders Woman to Stay in Hantavirus Quarantine, Despite C.D.C. Recommendation
item.description: Others who were in the facility have been allowed to return to their homes in recent weeks for monitoring by local health officials.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

item.title: Alabama and Georgia Runoff Elections Offer a New Test of Trump’s Influence Tomorrow
item.description: The Peach State, as well as Alabama, will offer new tests of Trump’s influence.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:credit: Audra Melton for The New York Times

item.title: Will the Iran Deal Stick?
item.description: After many false starts, this cease-fire plan could be different. That’s because the war really has become painful for both the U.S. and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times

item.title: Inside the Partisan Battle Over America’s 250th Birthday Celebrations
item.description: President Trump sidestepped a congressional commission and formed his own group to plan the festivities. The result is a deeply partisan celebration, disgruntled celebrities and plenty of confusion.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and Eileen Sullivan

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Construction on the National Mall in preparation for the Great American State Fair, part of America’s 250th birthday celebrations.
